title: Transaction Lifecycle and System Concepts
description: A flowchart illustrating the states of a database transaction, from its initiation to its final commit or abort, including the role of the system log and recovery manager.
relationships: A transaction begins and enters the Active state where read/write operations occur. It then moves to Partially Committed. If successful, it Commits, writes to the System Log, and is Terminated. If it fails, it moves to a Failed state, where the Recovery Manager uses the Log to roll back changes before the transaction is Terminated.
